Interactive Resources

Tools & Explainers

Not slides. Not PDFs. Each tool is interactive — built to make abstract healthcare data concepts concrete and learnable.

Tool

U.S. Healthcare Multi-Lens Map

U.S. state-level healthcare data across six lenses: patient, clinical, operations, payer, policy, and baseline context. One map, every angle.

State Map5 LensesInteractive
Tool

The Healthcare Iceberg Map

An interactive map of the full U.S. healthcare system. All 8 layers visible at once, from patient experience to the policy and financial forces underneath.

System Map8 LayersInteractive
Reference

Healthcare Vendor Directory

A searchable, filterable directory of major healthcare technology vendors. What sector they operate in, which layers of the system they touch, and whether their technology is proven, emerging, or cautionary.

VendorsAIHealthcare Tech
Tool

Hospital Operations Map

Interactive cross-section of a modern hospital. Every unit, every floor, every service line. Click any department to see who staffs it, how patients move through it, how it bills, and what happens when it breaks.

Hospital OpsDepartmentsInteractive
Lab

Clinical SQL Mystery

Learn the Patient → Encounter → Clinical Event data model by solving a real clinical mystery using SQL queries against an Epic-style database. Four cases, escalating difficulty. No experience needed.

SQLEHRBeginner-friendly
A living resource for clinical informatics, healthcare data architecture, and the real story behind the systems that run modern medicine.

Built by David Eitel, RRT, MHA, MSRT, RRT-ACCS
Explore

Tools

Active development
Source Policy
All vendor-specific terminology, technical specifications, and system references on this site are derived from publicly available documentation — including fhir.epic.com, fhir.cerner.com, HL7 FHIR R4, ONC HealthIT.gov, and HHS.gov. Each factual claim is linked to its source. This site does not reproduce proprietary materials, internal vendor training content, or licensed documentation.
